output:
CVE-2016-15008 is a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the oxguy3 coebot-www application, specifically found in the JavaScript file js/channel.js. The vulnerability stems from improper neutralization of user input within several display functions, making the application susceptible to XSS attacks that can be initiated remotely. This flaw is classified under CWE-79, with a severity level of MEDIUM.

The impact of this vulnerability could allow attackers to execute malicious scripts in the context of affected users’ sessions, potentially leading to session hijacking or unauthorized actions. The vulnerable software is coebot-www, versions before July 13, 2016.

To mitigate this vulnerability, it is recommended to apply the patch identified as c1a6c44092585da4236237e0e7da94ee2996a0ca. Adhering to mitigation techniques such as M1048 and M1037 can further enhance security posture. This vulnerability is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1190.
